title: Algorithms & Pseudocode
subtitle: Think Step by Step!
overview: This session introduces students to Algorithms and Pseudocode using fun real-life examples. Students learn how computers follow step-by-step instructions to solve problems and how programmers plan solutions before coding.

images._note: Add photos from the actual session — classroom activities, whiteboard notes, students arranging steps, algorithm games, flowchart drawings.

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Students arranging paper steps in correct order
gallery.caption: Algorithms are step-by-step instructions
Making tea is an everyday algorithm

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Whiteboard photo of pseudocode example
gallery.caption: Writing logic before coding

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Robot instruction classroom game photo
gallery.caption: Give correct instructions to the robot!

gallery._placeholder: ADD: Student writing algorithm in notebook
gallery.caption: Programmers plan before coding

keyTopics: What is an Algorithm? — Step-by-step instructions to solve a problem
Real-life Algorithms — Brushing teeth, making tea, going to school
Importance of Correct Order in Instructions
What is Pseudocode? — Simple language used before coding

activity.title: Human Robot Algorithm Game
steps: Select one student to act as a robot
Robot follows instructions exactly as spoken
Students observe mistakes caused by unclear instructions

options: A step-by-step method to solve a problem
quiz.explanation: An algorithm is a set of steps followed to solve a problem correctly.

quiz.q: Which of the following is an example of an algorithm?
quiz.explanation: Making tea follows specific steps, so it is an algorithm.

quiz.q: Why is the correct order important in an algorithm?
quiz.explanation: Wrong order can give wrong results.

options: Simple language used to plan programs
quiz.explanation: Pseudocode helps programmers think before coding.

quiz.q: Which comes first while solving a problem?
quiz.explanation: Good programmers first plan the solution.
Computers follow instructions exactly as given.
Flowcharts show steps visually using symbols.

quiz.q: Which of these is NOT an algorithm?
What skill improves by learning algorithms?
quiz.explanation: Algorithms improve logical and problem-solving skills.

quiz.q: What happens if instructions are unclear?
quiz.explanation: Incorrect or unclear instructions lead to mistakes.

quiz.q: Which statement is an example of pseudocode?
quiz.explanation: START is commonly used in pseudocode.
Pseudocode makes coding easier and organized.
Algorithms should be simple and clear.

quiz.q: What did students learn from the Robot Game activity?
quiz.explanation: The activity showed the importance of giving proper instructions.
